state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Washington combines maritime perennial crops with interior dryland grains—Roza and Wenatchee districts set global fruit benchmarks.
fun_facts: Washington produces the majority of U.S. apples most years.
Yakima Valley hops supply craft brewers worldwide.

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Oregon exports grass seed and specialty seed crops while Willamette Valley berries rival Washington tonnage.
fun_facts: Hood River fruit loop orchards draw tourists each pear and cherry season.

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Idaho’s branded russets anchor global french fry supply chains; Magic Valley dairies support regional cheese.
fun_facts: Idaho produces about a third of U.S. potatoes by weight.
Snake River irrigation lifts desert acres into intensive production.

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Alaska’s farm sector is tiny by acreage but culturally vital—long daylight midsummer compresses growth into weeks.
fun_facts: Giant cabbages at the Alaska State Fair exploit extreme photoperiods.
Subsistence fisheries remain central to rural food security.
